title: Wright to Drive No. 99 RFK Mustang at Goodwood; Kevu to Pilot 2020 AlphaTauri
meta: Published: 28 March 2026 | By Isabella Rossi, Breaking News & Live Reporter | F1CC News
caption: Lukus Wright will bring his NASCAR experience to Goodwood in the No. 99 RFK Mustang.
body:
GOODWOOD — Lukus Wright and Quarter Kevu will both take part in the Goodwood Festival of Speed next weekend, with Wright scheduled to drive the No. 99 RFK Racing Ford Mustang up the famous hillclimb. Kevu will be behind the wheel of the 2020 AlphaTauri AT01, the car that launched his Formula 1 career.

Wright confirmed the appearances in a social media post on Saturday morning. "I'll be heading to the Goodwood Festival of Speed next week while Formula 1 is out of town for the weekend," Wright wrote. "I'm scheduled to drive the No. 99 RFK Mustang up the hill and we'll make sure to put on a good show and let the crate motor sing a little."

Kevu, the reigning F1CC champion, will drive the 2020 AlphaTauri car — now rebranded as VCARB — that he raced during his debut season. "Quarter Kevu will also be there driving the 2020 AlphaTauri car, which should sound pretty healthy going through the hillclimb as well," Wright added.

The VCARB team will also have a significant presence at the event. "The team will also have the VCARB HQ set up in the Goodwood paddock throughout the weekend, so fans coming out will be able to spend some time around the team and check everything out while we're there," Wright said.

Wright's appearance comes less than two months after his NASCAR Cup Series debut at the Daytona 500, where he finished seventh driving the same No. 99 RFK Racing Ford. The Goodwood run will be his first public demonstration of stock car machinery since that race.

Kevu's AT01 is the car that carried him through the pandemic‑affected 2020 season, his first full campaign in Formula 1. The AlphaTauri brand has since been rebranded to Visa Cash App RB, but the 2020 livery remains a fan favourite.

Both drivers are currently competing in the 2026 F1CC championship. Wright sits seventh in the standings with 14 points after a challenging start. Kevu is second with 27 points, trailing only Ferrari's Eetu Väisänen on 57.

The Goodwood Festival of Speed runs from 3–6 April, falling on a weekend without an F1CC race. The championship resumes with the Japanese Grand Prix at Suzuka on 12 April.

"Looking forward to seeing everyone there and catching up with some people from around the paddock again," Wright wrote. "Should be a good weekend."

What it means: Goodwood continues to attract current F1 talent during the championship break. With Jack TEF and Eren Aygen also confirmed for the event, and Robin Keisner scheduled to drive the Audi R10 TDI, the Festival will host an unusually strong gathering of 2026 grid members.

Last updated: 09:30 BST, 28 March 2026

[More to follow]
